challenges.title: Linear Search Function
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that defines a function called `linearSearch` which takes an int array, its size, and a target value as parameters. The function should return the index of the target if found, or -1 if not found. In `main`, create an array of at least 5 integers, call `linearSearch` to find a value, and print the result. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'linearSearch' in cpp, 'You must define a function called linearSearch'
assert 'int ' in cpp and '[]' in cpp or 'int*' in cpp or 'int *' in cpp, 'linearSearch should accept an int array parameter'
assert 'return -1' in cpp or 'return(-1)' in cpp, 'Function should return -1 when the target is not found'
assert 'for' in cpp or 'while' in cpp, 'Use a loop to search through the array'
assert cpp.count('return') >= 2, 'Function needs at least two return statements (found index and not-found case)'
hints: The function signature should be: int linearSearch(int arr[], int size, int target)
Loop through the array comparing each element to the target value

challenges.title: Bubble Sort with Nested Loops
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that defines a `bubbleSort` function to sort an int array in ascending order using nested loops. The function should take an int array and its size as parameters. In `main`, create an array of at least 5 unsorted integers, call `bubbleSort`, then print the sorted array. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'bubbleSort' in cpp, 'You must define a function called bubbleSort'
assert cpp.count('for') >= 2 or (cpp.count('while') >= 2) or ('for' in cpp and 'while' in cpp), 'Bubble sort requires nested loops (two loops)'
assert 'swap' in cpp or 'temp' in cpp or 'tmp' in cpp, 'You need a swap mechanism (use a temp variable or std::swap)'
assert 'void' in cpp, 'bubbleSort should return void since it modifies the array in place'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Print the sorted array to verify the result'
hints: The outer loop controls the number of passes; the inner loop compares adjacent elements
To swap two values, use a temporary variable: int temp = arr[i]; arr[i] = arr[j]; arr[j] = temp;
